story.text: As bdo (and any session), I need to know at a glance whether every expected standing process is actually running, what is scheduled, and what is live right now for both of us, because the loop tick I just stood up can die silently and nobody would know — an unmonitored automation is a liability.

briefing.headline: A session-start watchdog: are the standing processes up, what's scheduled, what's live
briefing.value: You stood up the loop-tick scheduler and immediately asked the right question — how do we both know if it dies? This is the watchdog: at each session start it checks every declared standing process (registered + enabled + ran recently), shows the schedule, and shows what's live for both of us (recent log activity by actor). A down process screams ambiently.
briefing.why_now: An unattended scheduled task that spends money and advances work MUST be monitored — a silent death is the worst failure. This closes that the moment the automation exists.
briefing.if_accepted: Every standing process is declared in .claude/expected-processes.json and watched; a new one adds a registry entry; both of us see a DOWN at the next session start.
briefing.if_rejected: The loop tick (and any future standing process) can die silently and the queue quietly stops moving again — the exact problem that prompted the automation.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: loop/health.py is a read-only fold + a guarded schtasks probe (degrades off Windows); it writes nothing. The only wiring is one fail-open SessionStart hook. Reverting is removing the hook line.
briefing.mechanism: loop/health.py reads .claude/expected-processes.json; per process probes its OS task (schtasks) and freshness file mtime -> up/down; schedule() lists next-runs; live() folds the log's last 45m by actor. --hook mode speaks only when something is DOWN (fail-open, exit 0). Declared in the activity register (the teeth caught it as an undeclared collector first).

source_artifacts: bdo 2026-06-22: 'ALERTS for when expected registered processes are not running, a health check at each claude session start ... show our schedule and what's currently live for both of us'
